title: Cambridge CAE 3 — Test 3 (Reading & Use of English)

sections.title: Part 1: Multiple Choice (Reading)
sections.instructions: You are going to read three extracts which are concerned in some way with the arts or lifestyle. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text.
sections.passageHtml:
Extract 1: An Actor’s Life Story

Me: Stories of My Life is Katharine Hepburn’s account of her upbringing, her long career in film and theater, and her intriguing private life. This autobiography is a pleasure to read because it’s punchy, courageous, and intimate. She begins her story with the history of her close-knit, progressive family and goes through her school days — acting in the school plays and getting suspended. Her description of her early career in theater is fascinating, as she was fired by nearly every person who employed her. She was ‘very young and absolutely outrageous and full of a sort of wild confidence based on nothing but energy and ego’. Audiences had mixed responses. Ironically, male producers were the ones to fire her, and their wives always chastized them, saying that someday she was going to be a big star.

At least, that’s how Hepburn puts it. She details both her triumphs and failures, and she manages a charming combination of rampant self-worship and witty humility, but there’s no objectivity here. And that makes it all the better. Her descriptions of her life and work are anecdotal — stories from the set, and details about which producers treated her well and which badly. She is a great storyteller and indulges our curiosity without compromising her integrity. Her autobiography deals with several layers, including her relationship with ‘that creature whom I created’, her onscreen alter ego. It’s a fascinating look at a spirited misfit’s rise to fame, as much a cultural study of the entertainment industry as it is a personal psychological journey.

Extract 2: Glastonbury

Film director Julien Temple’s career has contained more ups and downs than a day at a theme park, but things are most definitely in the ascendancy with Glastonbury. A magical documentary recounting the 35 tumultuous years of the Glastonbury Music Festival, this is an exhilarating experience — I would say, nearly as good as being at the festival itself. Drawing upon 900 hours of footage submitted by the public, 450 hours shot by Temple himself, broadcast concert material and archival news coverage, Glastonbury is a sprawling affair that succeeds because it captures the spirit of the festival itself: rough rather than polished; organic rather than manufactured.

For the most part Temple tells his story chronologically, tracing the festival’s roots as a hippie gathering in the 1970s, through a politically active period in the early 1980s, and an involvement with alternative lifestyles in the late 1980s. It is noteworthy that, as the event becomes less about counter-culture and more about product placement, the documentary’s predominant background noise becomes mobile ringtones. As well as providing a fascinating social snapshot of festival goers, Glastonbury is also a celebration of the hundreds of bands who've played the festival over the years. Temple does not make the mistake of overloading the film with music, and selects his acts carefully. Most represent particularly memorable moments in the festival’s history, in particular Pulp’s storming rendition of Common People in the 1990s. Put simply, Glastonbury rocks.

Extract 3: Writing for the Stage

A playwright creates scripts and tells stories through the words and actions of characters. While the output of the playwright can stand on its own as literature, its potential is fully realised only when the skills of all the other theatre artists combine to transform the script into a production of a play.

A playwright’s tool kit needs to contain:
• a vivid imagination
• a gripping story to tell
• insight into what makes people tick
• a good ear for capturing authentic dialogue
• working knowledge of the elements of dramatic structure
• an understanding of theatre’s non-verbal means of expression — sound, movement, setting, costume, lighting, music, pacing and stage picture.

Playwrights sometimes bring actors and directors into the task of script creation in a valuable process known as collective creation. Here everyone takes a hand in researching the narrative and developing the script. But because the skilful ordering and re-creation of events is central to the playwright’s art, the final task of shaping the raw material into a coherent playscript is normally the playwright’s responsibility. Playwrights may also develop a new script with guidance from a dramaturg, who is a kind of editor. A dramaturg’s feedback on a new script may be given at any point in the process, from the first draft through to rehearsals. One way of doing this is through workshops, organised by the dramaturg, which involve a reading or scene study of the script by a director and professional actors. A new script is often strengthened by the feedback the playwright receives through this process.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose the correct letter A, B, C or D.

questions.instruction: 1. Why does the reviewer find Katharine Hepburn’s early acting career interesting?
questions.explanation: Chọn C — 'She often lost jobs in the theatre.' Văn bản viết: 'Her description of her early career in theater is fascinating, as she was fired by nearly every person who employed her.' (Việc cô bị sa thải bởi hầu hết mọi người tuyển dụng mình làm cho giai đoạn đầu sự nghiệp trở nên thú vị/hấp dẫn). Do đó, 'lost jobs' đồng nghĩa với 'was fired'. ❌ Bẫy & Phân tích phương án nhiễu: - A sai vì thực tế các nam nhà sản xuất sa thải cô chứ không phải nhận ra khí chất ngôi sao để tuyển dụng ('male producers were the ones to fire her'), còn vợ của họ mới là người chỉ trích chồng và nói cô sẽ thành sao lớn ('their wives always chastized them, saying that someday she was going to be a big star'). - B sai vì phản ứng của khán giả là trái chiều/hỗn hợp chứ không phải hoàn toàn phản đối ('Audiences had mixed responses'). - D sai vì văn bản chỉ nói cô có sự tự tin hoang dại ('wild confidence based on nothing but energy and ego') chứ không so sánh cô với các diễn viên lớn tuổi khác. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 35. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: C. She often lost jobs in the theatre.. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. Producers recognised that she had star quality.
B. Audiences disapproved of her acting style.
C. She often lost jobs in the theatre.
D. She had more self-confidence than older actors.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 1: Multiple Choice (Reading)

questions.instruction: 2. What does the reviewer suggest when he writes: ‘At least, that’s how Hepburn puts it.’ in line 9?
questions.explanation: Chọn C — 'Her account is one-sided.' Cụm từ 'At least, that's how Hepburn puts it' (Ít nhất thì đó là cách Hepburn trình bày) ngụ ý rằng câu chuyện được kể hoàn toàn theo góc nhìn chủ quan, phiến diện của tác giả mà thiếu đi tính khách quan. Câu tiếp theo củng cố điều này: 'but there’s no objectivity here' (không có sự khách quan nào ở đây cả). Do đó, tài liệu mang tính một chiều (one-sided). ❌ Bẫy & Phân tích phương án nhiễu: - A sai vì đây là lời khen ở câu sau ('She is a great storyteller'), không phải là ẩn ý của cụm từ 'At least...'. - B sai vì cụm từ tập trung vào độ tin cậy và tính khách quan của thông tin chứ không phải phong cách viết có hào hứng hay không. - D sai vì cô viết về cả người đối xử tốt và xấu ('which producers treated her well and which badly') chứ mục đích chính không phải chỉ để chỉ trích các nhà sản xuất. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 35. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: C. Her account is one-sided.. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. She is a very good storyteller.
B. Her style of writing is very exciting.
D. She uses the book to criticise producers.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 1: Multiple Choice (Reading)

questions.instruction: 3. The writer particularly enjoyed Glastonbury because the film
questions.explanation: Chọn B — 'reflects the character of its subject.' Tác giả viết: 'Glastonbury is a sprawling affair that succeeds because it captures the spirit of the festival itself: rough rather than polished; organic rather than manufactured.' (Bộ phim thành công vì nó ghi lại được tinh thần/tính chất của chính lễ hội: xù xì thô ráp chứ không bóng bẩy, tự nhiên chứ không phải được sản xuất hàng loạt). 'Character of its subject' tương ứng với 'spirit of the festival itself'. ❌ Bẫy & Phân tích phương án nhiễu: - A sai vì văn bản không đề cập đến kỹ năng biên tập phim (editing skill) mà nhấn mạnh việc sử dụng nguồn tư liệu phong phú từ công chúng và bản thân đạo diễn. - C sai vì tác giả chỉ nói sự nghiệp của Julien Temple có nhiều thăng trầm chứ không bày tỏ sự ngưỡng mộ cá nhân với đạo diễn này. - D sai vì mặc dù phim sử dụng 900 giờ tư liệu từ công chúng, tác giả không lấy việc nhìn thấy cảnh quay mới lạ làm lý do chính cho sự yêu thích của mình. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 36. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: B. reflects the character of its subject.. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: B. reflects the character of its subject.
C. is made by a director he admires.
D. contains footage he has not seen before.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 1: Multiple Choice (Reading)

questions.instruction: 4. What significant change in the festival is highlighted by the film?
questions.explanation: Chọn D — 'The festival is run along more commercial lines these days.' Tác giả chỉ ra sự thay đổi lớn: 'as the event becomes less about counter-culture and more about product placement, the documentary’s predominant background noise becomes mobile ringtones.' Việc chuyển từ văn hóa phản kháng (counter-culture) sang quảng cáo sản phẩm (product placement) và tiếng nhạc chuông điện thoại làm nền cho thấy lễ hội ngày càng mang tính thương mại hóa cao hơn. ❌ Bẫy & Phân tích phương án nhiễu: - A sai vì văn bản không nhắc đến quy mô khán giả tăng lên. - B sai vì không có thông tin về số lượng ban nhạc tham gia tăng qua mỗi năm. - C sai vì văn bản chỉ nói phim tôn vinh hàng trăm ban nhạc đã biểu diễn chứ không nói về việc thu hút nhiều ban nhạc nổi tiếng hơn trước. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 36. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: D. The festival is run along more commercial lines these days.. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. The audiences are much larger than in the past.
B. An increasing number of bands are playing each year.
C. The festival attracts more celebrity bands than previously.
D. The festival is run along more commercial lines these days.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 1: Multiple Choice (Reading)

questions.instruction: 5. What is the writer’s main purpose in this text?
questions.explanation: Chọn B — 'to describe what a playwright’s job involves.' Bài viết nêu rõ định nghĩa về nhà viết kịch, liệt kê các công cụ cần có ('playwright’s tool kit'), và mô tả quy trình sáng tác kịch bản từ khâu hợp tác tập thể ('collective creation') đến việc làm việc với cố vấn kịch bản ('dramaturg'). Điều này nhằm phác họa những gì mà công việc của một nhà viết kịch bao gồm. ❌ Bẫy & Phân tích phương án nhiễu: - A sai vì bài viết không tập trung vào những khó khăn hay trở ngại của công việc này mà chỉ nêu các yêu cầu chuyên môn và quy trình làm việc. - C sai vì giọng văn mang tính thông tin, giải thích khách quan chứ không mang tính thuyết phục hay quảng bá. - D sai vì bài viết chỉ đề cập đến sự kết hợp giữa các nghệ sĩ khác để hiện thực hóa kịch bản, chứ không đi sâu vào so sánh hay phân biệt vai trò của diễn viên và nhà viết kịch. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 36. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: B. to describe what a playwright’s job involves.. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. to outline the difficulties of writing plays.
B. to describe what a playwright’s job involves.
C. to persuade people to write for the theatre.
D. to distinguish between actors and playwrights.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 1: Multiple Choice (Reading)

questions.instruction: 6. In the writer’s opinion, a theatrical script
questions.explanation: Chọn C — 'can usefully be created by a group of people.' Tác giả viết: 'Playwrights sometimes bring actors and directors into the task of script creation in a valuable process known as collective creation.' Cụm từ 'valuable process' (quy trình có giá trị) cho thấy tác giả đánh giá cao tính hữu ích của việc đồng sáng tạo kịch bản bởi một nhóm người (gồm nhà viết kịch, diễn viên và đạo diễn). ❌ Bẫy & Phân tích phương án nhiễu: - A sai vì dramaturg chỉ đóng vai trò hỗ trợ và đưa ra phản hồi ('guidance/feedback') chứ không quyết định sự thành bại dựa trên sự phê duyệt (approval). - B sai vì tác giả cho rằng kịch bản chỉ thực sự phát huy hết tiềm năng khi được kết hợp với các nghệ sĩ khác trên sân khấu chứ không hề bị giảm tác động. - D sai vì tác giả viết 'the output of the playwright can stand on its own as literature' chứ không nói nó hiếm khi được đánh giá cao như văn học. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 36. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: C. can usefully be created by a group of people.. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. often depends for its success on the approval of a dramaturg.
B. will lose some of its impact during the production process.
C. can usefully be created by a group of people.
D. is rarely as highly regarded as a piece of literature.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 1: Multiple Choice (Reading)

sections.title: Part 2: Gapped Text (Reading)
sections.instructions: You are going to read an extract from a magazine article about psychology. Six paragraphs have been removed from the extract. Choose from the paragraphs A-G the one which fits each gap (7-12). There is one extra paragraph which you do not need to use.
sections.passageHtml:
Human Behaviour

Peter Jones reports on how groups function.

Unless you're a loner, you're probably a member of many different groups. Some are long lasting, like your family, while others, such as a football crowd, are temporary. Every group functions in different ways but there are still some common psychological features to any group.

[ Gap 7 ]

Being part of a group changes the way you behave. The presence of others has a generally arousing effect on the nervous system. This is natural — you don’t know what they will do. They may move about, speak to you, or even attack you, and your brain has to pay attention to all these messages. Having others around is just distracting. It divides your attention, so you can’t focus on the task in hand.

[ Gap 8 ]

But when it comes to complex tasks, the prospect of not being evaluated may free you from anxiety, so encouraging you to perform better. To test this, psychologists asked volunteers to carry out a complex computer task in separate rooms. Some were told performance would be evaluated individually - causing performance anxiety — while others were told the results would be averaged with the rest of the group. As expected, the second group did better than the first.

The problem becomes even worse when individuals are disguised with war paint or uniforms. Analysis shows that the more people there are in a mob, the greater the antisocial behaviour. Being ‘submerged’ within a group

[ Gap 9 ]

does have many negative connotations but it’s not always a bad thing. Instead of thinking about a rowdy mob, think of relaxing, dancing and enjoying yourself at a party where you are also just a part of the crowd.

[ Gap 10 ]

But surprisingly, research has shown that the use of this technique by groups isn’t that effective in either the number or quality of ideas generated. You get better results if you set people to work individually on a problem. We also tend to assume that decisions made by groups are better than those made by individuals, which is why we form committees.

[ Gap 11 ]

But any group can get a decision badly wrong, because their thought processes can go awry. This is a process psychologists call ‘groupthink’. A close-knit group of advisers isolated from argument and criticism can grow to believe they can do no wrong. The group then becomes more important than the individuals who are part of it. Further, a leadership style which concentrates on the group rather than the individual can contribute to this.

[ Gap 12 ]

Many, but not all, leaders show dominance in the group. But the dominant person in the group doesn’t always make the best leader, and it isn’t necessarily true that having the smartest people always makes for the most successful group.

Paragraph Options (A–G):

A. This lack of individual accountability may lead to people letting go of their inhibitions — a process known as ‘deindividuation’. Sometimes this is dangerous, releasing violent and impulsive behaviours that individuals would never dream of indulging in alone.

B. They have a need for power, characterised by talking a lot, wanting to be listened to and to make decisions. In a group it can be easy to spot the dominant people. They make eye contact, point and even touch people but don’t encourage return gestures. Such behaviour studies have been key in shaping business organisation.

C. For example, in general, humans are social animals, that’s why we get together in groups in the first place. Important elements of our individual identity come from being part of a group. Most people enjoy being in a group — it’s a way of forming emotionally satisfying relationships.

D. But it has to be a carefully selected team, not a randomly generated group, if creative decisions are to be made. In fact, theory on team building has shown that it is better if people work in small teams of complementary pairs. Big teams don’t get anything done, even though people like them.

E. For a group and its behaviour are shaped by who is in charge and the roles the other members play. A good front man or woman is persuasive, not directive, communicates and speaks clearly, listens well and appeals to group members’ emotions and feelings as well as thoughts and ideas.

F. So, how does all this stimulation affect achievement? It has been argued that people do better on simple well-rehearsed activities when they’re with others than when they are alone. Also, if their individual efforts within the group are not being monitored, there’s a tendency to relax and merge into the crowd.

G. Another positive feature of groups is that they generate ideas and opinions, and use these to make decisions. That’s why the modern trend in teaching is for students to work in small groups to prepare presentations and why brainstorming is so popular in the work context.

questionGroups.instruction: Select the correct paragraph (A–G) for each gap.

questions.explanation: Chọn C. Đoạn mở đầu giới thiệu về các loại nhóm khác nhau và khẳng định mọi nhóm đều có một số đặc điểm tâm lý chung ('common psychological features'). Đoạn C bắt đầu bằng 'For example, in general, humans are social animals, that’s why we get together in groups in the first place.' (Ví dụ, nhìn chung, con người là động vật xã hội, đó là lý do tại sao chúng ta tập hợp thành nhóm ngay từ đầu). Điều này bổ sung ví dụ trực tiếp cho 'common psychological features' ở đoạn trước. ⚠️ Bẫy: Nhiều học sinh bị bẫy chọn G vì nghĩ 'generate ideas' là đặc điểm tâm lý chung của nhóm, nhưng đoạn sau Gap 7 tập trung vào sự thay đổi hành vi cá nhân ('changes the way you behave'), không liên quan đến việc đưa ra quyết định của nhóm. Note: Trong tài liệu đáp án gốc (Test 3 Key), câu này bị ghi nhầm ký tự thành '7G' do lỗi nhận diện quang học OCR giữa chữ 'C' và chữ 'G' (do hai chữ cái này rất giống nhau), tuy nhiên dựa trên mạch văn logic của đề bài, đáp án chính xác bắt buộc phải là C. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 37. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: C. Answer-key option. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 2: Gapped Text (Reading)

questions.explanation: Chọn F. Đoạn trước Gap 8 thảo luận về tác động làm phân tán sự chú ý của người khác ('divides your attention, so you can’t focus on the task in hand'). Đoạn F nối tiếp tự nhiên bằng câu hỏi: 'So, how does all this stimulation affect achievement?' (Vậy, tất cả sự kích thích này ảnh hưởng thế nào đến thành tích?) và đưa ra luận điểm về nhiệm vụ đơn giản ('do better on simple well-rehearsed activities'). Đoạn sau Gap 8 bắt đầu bằng 'But when it comes to complex tasks...' (Nhưng khi nói đến các nhiệm vụ phức tạp...) tạo nên sự tương phản trực tiếp hoàn hảo với các nhiệm vụ đơn giản ở đoạn F. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 37. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: F. Answer-key option. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 2: Gapped Text (Reading)

questions.explanation: Chọn A. Đoạn trước Gap 9 mô tả hiện tượng con người ẩn mình trong đám đông (uniforms, war paint, mob) dẫn đến hành vi chống đối xã hội tăng lên ('antisocial behaviour. Being ‘submerged’ within a group'). Đoạn A tiếp nối và định nghĩa hiện tượng này: 'This lack of individual accountability may lead to people letting go of their inhibitions — a process known as ‘deindividuation’.' (Sự thiếu trách nhiệm giải trình cá nhân này dẫn đến việc buông bỏ các rào cản kiểm soát hành vi - quá trình phi cá nhân hóa). Câu tiếp theo sau Gap 9 là: 'does have many negative connotations but it’s not always a bad thing.' liên kết trực tiếp với các hành vi nguy hiểm, bốc đồng ('dangerous, releasing violent and impulsive behaviours') được mô tả ở cuối đoạn A. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 37. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: A. Answer-key option. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 2: Gapped Text (Reading)

questions.explanation: Chọn G. Đoạn sau Gap 10 bắt đầu bằng: 'But surprisingly, research has shown that the use of this technique by groups isn’t that effective in either the number or quality of ideas generated. You get better results if you set people to work individually on a problem...' Cụm 'this technique' (kỹ thuật này) buộc phải tham chiếu đến một phương pháp được đề cập ngay trước đó. Đoạn G thảo luận về việc thảo luận nhóm và nêu rõ lý do tại sao kỹ thuật động não ('brainstorming') lại phổ biến trong công việc. Kỹ thuật động não này chính là 'this technique' ở câu sau. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 37. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. questions.explanation: Chọn D. Đoạn trước Gap 11 nói về xu hướng cho rằng quyết định nhóm tốt hơn quyết định cá nhân, dẫn đến việc thành lập các ủy ban ('which is why we form committees'). Đoạn D đưa ra phản biện trực tiếp: 'But it has to be a carefully selected team, not a randomly generated group, if creative decisions are to be made.' (Nhưng đó phải là một đội ngũ được lựa chọn kỹ lưỡng, chứ không phải một nhóm ngẫu nhiên, nếu muốn đưa ra các quyết định sáng tạo). Đồng thời, đoạn sau Gap 11 tiếp tục phản biện về các quyết định sai lầm của nhóm ('But any group can get a decision badly wrong, because their thought processes can go awry'). Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 37. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. questions.explanation: Chọn E. Đoạn trước Gap 12 kết luận bằng ý: 'Further, a leadership style which concentrates on the group rather than the individual can contribute to this.' (Hơn nữa, một phong cách lãnh đạo tập trung vào nhóm hơn là cá nhân có thể góp phần vào việc này). (Vì một nhóm và hành vi của nó được định hình bởi người đứng đầu và vai trò của các thành viên khác). sections.title: Part 3: Multiple Choice (Reading)
sections.instructions: You are going to read a magazine article about an ancient Greek shipwreck. For questions 13-19, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text.
sections.passageHtml:
Discovering the secrets of an ancient Greek shipwreck

John Green reports on a recent find in the Mediterranean.

While other members of my team explored the wreck of a small Greek merchant ship that sank off the Turkish coast more than 2,400 years ago, I hovered above them in a submersible. One diver, an archaeologist, placed an amphora, a two-handled jar, inside a lifting basket. Another vacuumed sediment from the site by fanning sand into the mouth of a nearly vertical suction pipe. Two more were taking measurements, carefully, but of necessity quickly; for at this depth each diver had only 20 minutes to complete the morning's assigned task. Any longer, and they would require lengthy decompression, to avoid the divers’ ailment known as the bends.

In four decades of diving on shipwrecks, I'd been too engrossed in carrying out similar tasks to think of the families whose loved ones may have disappeared long ago. I had always concentrated on the technical features of my trade. I had stopped diving regularly 15 years before this exploration, turning over the bulk of the underwater work to a younger generation, but I continue to make inspection dives on most wrecks we excavate.

This was not just any wreck. Although I've been involved in uncovering the remains of much older ships, and of more than a hundred ancient shipwrecks along the Turkish coast, I had never even seen a wreck from the fifth century BC. Preliminary photographs of the cargo dated it to the third quarter of the century, during the Golden Age of classical Greece. Was there any other time when architecture, philosophy, sculpture, drama and politics reached near perfection in so few years? Athens, then as now the major city in Greece, controlled an empire stretching from one side of the Aegean Sea to the other. None of this would have been possible without naval might and maritime commerce. Athens could not even feed its own people without the grain imported by sea.

Back in the present, I brought the submersible up to the surface and returned to our camp, a summer home for our team of upwards of 30 people. Construction had been a major challenge. While looking for a suitable location I had struggled on that cape, which centuries of rough seas have eroded into sharp teeth of solid rock, but the long-term advantage of quartering in optimum proximity to the exploration site outweighed the short-term disadvantage of spending two months transforming the inhospitable rock into a comfortable camp.

During our three-year exploration of the wreck we excavated examples of nearly every type of jar that the classical Greeks made for wine or water. Many types — particularly the cheaper ones — might have been used as tableware by the ship's crew, but they were far in excess of what would have been required. We concluded therefore that they must have been cargo. Almost all seem to have been manufactured on the island of Chios, close to where the ship sank. We also discovered in the seabed two marble discs, which we guessed were the ship's eyes. It has long been known from vase paintings that classical Greek ships — like those from other cultures — had eyes to give them life or help them see their way through the waves. Although warships were known to have had naturalistic marble eyes attached to them, most scholars assumed that the eyes on more modest merchant ships were depicted as simple circles painted onto the sides of the vessel. Our marble eyes, the first from an actual ancient wreck and the first associated with a merchant ship, suggest otherwise. Not only were they made of marble, they had, like those of warships, been painted to show both the pupil and the iris.

Did the sailors who depended on these eyes for safety survive the ship's last voyage? They could have lived through the actual sinking. The ship was less than a hundred yards from land when it sank, so they might have swum towards the shore. And we know from Greek literature that some ships had lifeboats. But proximity to land and having lifeboats are no guarantees of safety. Even if some had swum to shore, it’s hard to imagine that many managed to crawl up on the exposed and sharp rocks while being smashed by waves like those that almost certainly sank their ship.

Several of our finds are important in the history of shipbuilding. The cargo, though, was not of great significance. There were the ceramic jars, most of them probably made along the coast where we were diving. A few came from north eastern Greece and were carrying a kind of pine tar used for purposes as varied as making wooden ships watertight and flavouring wine. The nature of the cargo suggests why the tools, weights, coins and personal possessions we usually find on ancient wrecks were absent: perhaps the ship made only day trips between Chios and nearby islands.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose the correct letter A, B, C or D.

questions.instruction: 13. In the first paragraph, what point does the writer make about the exploration?
questions.explanation: Chọn C — 'It had to take into account risks to the divers.' Trong đoạn 1, tác giả mô tả các thợ lặn làm việc cực kỳ cẩn thận và nhanh chóng ('carefully, but of necessity quickly') vì ở độ sâu này họ chỉ có 20 phút để hoàn thành nhiệm vụ. Nếu lâu hơn, họ sẽ phải trải qua quá trình giảm áp kéo dài để tránh căn bệnh nguy hiểm của thợ lặn gọi là 'the bends' (bệnh giảm áp do bọt khí nitơ trong máu). Điều này cho thấy cuộc thám hiểm phải cân nhắc rất kỹ các rủi ro sức khỏe của thợ lặn. ❌ Bẫy & Phương án nhiễu: - A sai vì văn bản không nói đội nhỏ hiệu quả hơn đội lớn, thực tế đội thám hiểm của họ lên đến hơn 30 người. - B sai vì mỗi thợ lặn được phân công một nhiệm vụ chuyên biệt (người khảo cổ đặt bình gốm, người hút trầm tích, người đo đạc) chứ không yêu cầu một người làm hết tất cả các kỹ năng. - D sai vì văn bản đề cập đến tàu ngầm nhỏ ('submersible') và ống hút cát ('suction pipe') nhưng không nhấn mạnh công nghệ làm cho công việc dễ dàng hơn mà nhấn mạnh áp lực thời gian và rủi ro. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 38. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: C. It had to take into account risks to the divers.. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. It was most effective when carried out by a small team.
B. It required each diver to possess a variety of skills.
C. It had to take into account risks to the divers.
D. It had been made easier by technological developments.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 3: Multiple Choice (Reading)

questions.instruction: 14. What does the writer suggest about himself in the second paragraph?
questions.explanation: Chọn D — 'He was aware he distanced himself from aspects of his work.' Trong đoạn 2, tác giả thừa nhận: 'In four decades of diving on shipwrecks, I'd been too engrossed in carrying out similar tasks to think of the families whose loved ones may have disappeared long ago. I had always concentrated on the technical features of my trade.' (Trong bốn thập kỷ lặn tìm xác tàu, tôi đã quá mải mê thực hiện các nhiệm vụ tương tự mà không nghĩ đến gia đình của những người có thể đã biến mất từ lâu. Tôi luôn chỉ tập trung vào các khía cạnh kỹ thuật của nghề nghiệp). Điều này thể hiện sự tự nhận thức của ông về việc mình đã cô lập/tách biệt bản thân khỏi các khía cạnh con người/cảm xúc của các vụ đắm tàu. ❌ Bẫy & Phân tích phương án nhiễu: - A sai vì ông chỉ nói mình tập trung vào khía cạnh kỹ thuật chuyên môn của mình chứ không tự mãn rằng mình có mọi kỹ năng cần thiết. - B sai vì ông chuyển giao công việc dưới nước cho thế hệ trẻ và chỉ lặn kiểm tra ('inspection dives') nhưng không nói điều này mang lại lợi ích cá nhân hay chuyên môn lớn nào ngoài việc giảm tải công việc. - C sai vì ông chỉ bàn giao công việc chứ không nói về việc đào tạo hay bày tỏ sự hài lòng về điều đó. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 38. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: D. He was aware he distanced himself from aspects of his work.. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. He had developed every skill that was needed for exploring wrecks.
B. He had benefited by changing his role in explorations.
C. He was pleased he had started training younger divers.
D. He was aware he distanced himself from aspects of his work.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 3: Multiple Choice (Reading)

questions.instruction: 15. The writer uses the words ‘not just any wreck’ in line 10 to imply that
questions.explanation: Chọn C — 'finding this particular ship was of exceptional significance.' Cụm từ 'not just any wreck' (không phải bất kỳ xác tàu nào) kết hợp với thông tin: 'Although I've been involved in uncovering... more than a hundred ancient shipwrecks..., I had never even seen a wreck from the fifth century BC.' (Mặc dù tôi đã tham gia khai quật hơn 100 xác tàu cổ, tôi chưa bao giờ nhìn thấy một xác tàu từ thế kỷ thứ 5 trước Công nguyên). Việc xác tàu thuộc về Thời Hoàng kim của Hy Lạp cổ đại ('Golden Age of classical Greece') cho thấy việc tìm thấy nó có ý nghĩa vô cùng đặc biệt và quan trọng. ❌ Bẫy & Phân tích phương án nhiễu: - A sai vì tác giả không nói mình đã tìm kiếm riêng xác tàu này từ lâu. - B sai vì mặc dù đây là sự thật (hơn 100 xác tàu khác đã được tìm thấy), cụm từ 'not just any wreck' nhằm nhấn mạnh giá trị đặc biệt của xác tàu này chứ không phải số lượng tàu khác. - D sai vì văn bản không đề cập đến việc xác tàu này còn nguyên vẹn hay ở tình trạng tốt hơn các tàu khác. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 38. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: C. finding this particular ship was of exceptional significance.. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. he had been searching for the ship for a considerable time.
B. this was not the only ship found off the coast of Turkey.
C. finding this particular ship was of exceptional significance.
D. the ship was in better condition than most wrecks.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 3: Multiple Choice (Reading)

questions.instruction: 16. What was the writer's priority for the camp?
questions.explanation: Chọn C — 'how near it was to the wreck'. Tác giả thảo luận về việc xây dựng trại trên một mũi đất đá lởm chởm hiểm trở ('inhospitable rock'). Ông chấp nhận thử thách và sự bất tiện đó vì: 'the long-term advantage of quartering in optimum proximity to the exploration site outweighed the short-term disadvantage of spending two months transforming the inhospitable rock into a comfortable camp.' (lợi thế lâu dài của việc đóng quân ở vị trí gần tối ưu nhất với địa điểm thám hiểm đã vượt qua những bất lợi ngắn hạn của việc xây dựng...). 'Optimum proximity' nghĩa là độ gần tối đa/vị trí sát bên địa điểm xác tàu. ❌ Bẫy & Phân tích phương án nhiễu: - A sai vì sự thoải mái chỉ là điều họ cố gắng khắc phục sau khi đã chọn vị trí hiểm trở chứ không phải ưu tiên hàng đầu. - B sai vì việc xây dựng thực tế rất khó khăn và mất đến 2 tháng ('spending two months transforming...'). - D sai vì đất ở đây rất hiểm trở và dốc đá, không phải là yếu tố diện tích đất có sẵn. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 38. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: C. how near it was to the wreck. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. how comfortable it could be made
B. how quickly it could be constructed
C. how near it was to the wreck
D. how much land was available
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 3: Multiple Choice (Reading)

questions.instruction: 17. The writer decided on the purpose of the jars on the basis that
questions.explanation: Chọn B — 'there was a larger quantity of them than the crew needed,'. Tác giải thích lý do họ kết luận các bình gốm (jars) là hàng hóa thương mại (cargo): 'Many types — particularly the cheaper ones — might have been used as tableware by the ship's crew, but they were far in excess of what would have been required. We concluded therefore that they must have been cargo.' (Nhiều loại bình có thể đã được thủy thủ đoàn dùng làm đồ ăn uống, nhưng chúng vượt quá xa số lượng cần thiết cho họ. Do đó chúng tôi kết luận chúng phải là hàng hóa). ❌ Bẫy & Phân tích phương án nhiễu: - A sai vì văn bản nói chúng là những loại rẻ tiền chứ không phải quá đắt giá. - C sai vì lý do kết luận là số lượng vượt quá nhu cầu ('far in excess') chứ không phải sự đa dạng về chủng loại. - D sai vì nguồn gốc xuất xứ ở Chios chỉ là thông tin phụ họa thêm, không phải căn cứ trực tiếp để phân biệt giữa đồ dùng của thủy thủ đoàn và hàng hóa thương mại. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 38. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: B. there was a larger quantity of them than the crew needed,. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. they were too valuable to be used on board ship.
B. there was a larger quantity of them than the crew needed,
C. there were more different types than the crew needed.
D. they were manufactured in Chios.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 3: Multiple Choice (Reading)

questions.instruction: 18. What was significant about the ship’s eyes that were found?
questions.explanation: Chọn A — 'It had been thought that eyes like these were only used on warships.' Văn bản viết: 'Although warships were known to have had naturalistic marble eyes attached to them, most scholars assumed that the eyes on more modest merchant ships were depicted as simple circles painted onto the sides... Our marble eyes, the first from an actual ancient wreck and the first associated with a merchant ship, suggest otherwise.' (Mặc dù tàu chiến được biết là có gắn mắt đá cẩm thạch mô phỏng tự nhiên, hầu hết các học giả cho rằng mắt trên các tàu buôn khiêm tốn chỉ là các vòng tròn đơn giản được vẽ lên... Những con mắt đá cẩm thạch của chúng tôi, lần đầu tiên tìm thấy từ một tàu buôn, đã gợi ra điều ngược lại). Điều này có nghĩa là trước đây người ta tin rằng mắt đá cẩm thạch tự nhiên chỉ dành cho tàu chiến. ❌ Bẫy & Phân tích phương án nhiễu: - B sai vì bình vẽ trên gốm chỉ cho thấy tàu có mắt chứ không mâu thuẫn hay khác biệt về mặt bản chất hình dáng được tìm thấy. - C sai vì vị trí của mắt để giúp tàu 'nhìn đường' là bình thường và đã được biết đến từ trước. - D sai vì văn bản không so sánh mắt của tàu Hy Lạp với các nền văn hóa khác để tìm sự tương đồng bất ngờ. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 38. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: A. It had been thought that eyes like these were only used on warships.. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. It had been thought that eyes like these were only used on warships.
B. They were different from the eyes on ships in vase paintings.
C. It was unusual for eyes to be placed in this position on a ship.
D. They were surprisingly similar to eyes on ships from other cultures.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 3: Multiple Choice (Reading)

questions.instruction: 19. What is the writer’s opinion about the chances of the sailors having survived?
questions.explanation: Chọn B — 'They would have found it difficult to climb onto land.' Tác giả nhận định về khả năng survival của các thủy thủ: 'Even if some had swum to shore, it’s hard to imagine that many managed to crawl up on the exposed and sharp rocks while being smashed by waves...' (Ngay cả khi một số người đã bơi được vào bờ, thật khó tưởng tượng nhiều người trong số họ có thể bò lên được những vách đá sắc nhọn nhô ra trong khi bị sóng đánh dữ dội). 'Crawl up' tương đương với 'climb onto land'. ❌ Bẫy & Phân tích phương án nhiễu: - A sai vì tàu chỉ cách bờ chưa đầy 100 yards nên họ hoàn toàn có thể bơi được. - C sai vì họ vẫn có thể bơi vào gần bờ, nhưng điểm nguy hiểm là việc leo lên bờ đá sắc nhọn dưới sức sóng đánh. - D sai vì văn bản chỉ nói một số tàu có xuồng cứu sinh chứ không khẳng định xuồng của họ bị sóng phá hủy hay họ có sử dụng chúng hay không. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 38. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: B. They would have found it difficult to climb onto land.. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. They would have been too far from land to swim ashore.
B. They would have found it difficult to climb onto land.
C. The sea would have been too rough to swim in.
D. Their lifeboats would have been destroyed by the waves.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 3: Multiple Choice (Reading)

sections.title: Part 4: Multiple Matching (Reading)
sections.instructions: You are going to read an article in which scientists and writers nominate their favourite science fiction authors. For questions 20-34, choose from the authors (A-D). The authors may be chosen more than once.
sections.passageHtml:
Favourite Science Fiction Authors

Top scientists and writers nominate their favourite science fiction authors

A. Isaac Asimov

Isaac Asimov, the founding father of modern science fiction, came out as a clear favourite among today’s scientists and writers. Trained as a chemist, Asimov also held a teaching post at Boston University for many years. ‘Although not as elegant a prose writer as many of his contemporaries, Asimov was, however, very rigorous scientifically, and thoughtful about how he projected scientific ideas into the future,’ says Philip Ball, a writer of popular science books. Two works mark Asimov out as the master of the genre: I, Robot, and the Foundation trilogy. In the Foundation series, science and maths were used to predict and plan the development of societies, something which Mark Brake, professor of science communication, thinks may be a touch farfetched: ‘We often can’t even predict a flood in a nearby town, let alone how a society behaves a thousand years in the future. Unlike a lot of sci-fi writers, Asimov knew how to explain the science, and was a keen populariser of real science,’ says Brake, ‘But what sets him apart is that he was also masterful at documenting human responses to scientific progress.’

B. John Wyndham

John Wyndham, author of The Day of the Triffids and many other extremely successful works, was one of the few science fiction writers to become hugely popular with people who never normally read science fiction. Julia Higgins, professor of polymer science, describes Wyndham's writing as ‘good novels in which there were real people, and the science issues simply pushed the real people into dramatic circumstances.’ Up until the late 1940s, sci-fi was almost exclusively set in space, and involved what Wyndham himself described as ‘the adventures of galactic gangsters’. Wyndham’s deliberate choice of innocuously ‘cosy’ English backdrops is central to the power of his novels, implying that apocalypse could occur at any time — or, indeed, be happening in the next village at this moment. Although Wyndham did not invent the English catastrophe novel, he re-established the genre and examined its themes with the freshness of a pioneer.

C. Fred Hoyle

One of Britain’s most creative scientists, Hoyle was as well known for his far-reaching work in the 1950s and 60s as a mathematician and astrophysicist as he was as a science fiction author. Hoyle felt that his science fiction writing was an important complement to his scientific career, and was convinced that major discoveries were most likely to come from an exercise of the creative imagination. He originated the notion that life arrived on earth by way of an asteroid striking the planet, a hypothesis which, although ridiculed at the time, has now earned respectable scientific credentials.

Hoyle wrote and co-authored a number of highly-regarded works including A for Andromeda: A Novel for Tomorrow (1962), and Ossian’s Ride (1959). But it is his first novel, The Black Cloud (1957), for which he is chiefly renowned as a sci-fi writer. Evolutionary biologist Richard Dawkins sees it as a major influential work: ‘In The Black Cloud I learned about scientific method and information theory — the interchangeability of different kinds of information.’ In the novel, humans try to communicate with an alien intelligence, in the form of a cloud of gas, by playing it piano music translated into radio signals. ‘Today we see information theory in genetic code and the translation of information from one computer to another,’ says Dawkins.

D. Philip K. Dick

Philip K. Dick is certainly one of the most adapted science fiction novelists: his 1968 novel Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep? became the seminal film Blade Runner, We Can Remember It For You Wholesale became the movie Total Recall and Minority Report was filmed by Steven Spielberg in 2002. But his cerebral works were underrated for years until Blade Runner brought acclaim — only months after his death. ‘The fact that what Dick is entertaining us about is sanity and madness, time and death, sin and salvation, has escaped most critics,’ says writer Ursula K. Le Guin.

‘Most of Dick’s books are concerned with the question of how we know what is real. These are the key questions for all of us who study the neural aspects of consciousness,’ says Chris Frith of University College London’s Institute of Cognitive Neuroscience. Dick's writing dealt with unsettling ideas such as the theft of memory and personality, and with what he saw as the false realities manufactured by the media, governments, and big corporations. Writer Robin McKie admires the remarkable foresight in Dick’s works: ‘Nowadays, when identities are stolen along with mobile phones and credit cards, when CCTVs scan our movements, and when the news is constantly being manipulated, Dick looks like an inspirational visionary.’

sections.instructions: For questions 1–12, read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).
sections.passageHtml:
Smart shoes

Smart shoes that (0) adjust their size throughout the day could soon be available. A prototype has already been produced and a commercial (1) ..... may be in production within a few years. The shoe contains sensors that constantly check the amount of (2) ..... left in it. If the foot has become too large, a tiny valve opens and the shoe (3) ..... slightly. The entire control system is about 5mm square and is located inside the shoe. This radical shoe (4) ..... a need because the volume of the (5) ..... foot can change by as much as 8% during the course of the day.

The system is able to learn about the wearer’s feet and (6) ..... up a picture of the size of his or her feet throughout the day. It will (7) ..... the shoes to change in size by up to 8% so that they always fit (8) ..... . They are obviously more comfortable and less likely to (9) ..... blisters. From an athlete’s point of view, they can help improve (10) ..... a little, and that is why the first (11) ..... for the system is likely to be in a sports shoe. Eventually, this system will find a (12) ..... in many other household items, from beds that automatically change to fit the person sleeping in them, to power tools that shape themselves to the user’s hand for better grip.

questions.explanation: Chọn B — 'version'. Collocation 'commercial version' = phiên bản thương mại (sản phẩm sẵn sàng bán ra thị trường). Bản mẫu (prototype) đã được làm ra, và phiên bản thương mại sẽ sớm được sản xuất. ❌ Bẫy & Phân tích phương án nhiễu: - A 'assortment' = sự phân loại, hỗn hợp nhiều loại (không đi kèm commercial trong ngữ cảnh này). - C 'style' = kiểu dáng, phong cách (không chỉ một phiên bản sản phẩm hoàn thiện). - D 'variety' = sự đa dạng, trạng thái nhiều loại (không phù hợp nghĩa sản phẩm). Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 71. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. questions.explanation: Chọn A — 'allow'. Cấu trúc 'allow somebody/something to do something' = cho phép ai/cái gì làm gì. Hệ thống tự động này cho phép đôi giày thay đổi kích thước. ❌ Bẫy & Phân tích phương án nhiễu: - B 'absolutely', C 'completely', D 'totally' không phải là động từ thích hợp đứng sau chủ ngữ 'It will'. (Đây là các trạng từ gây nhiễu nếu thí sinh nhầm cấu trúc câu). Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 71. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: A. allow. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.

questions.explanation: Chọn A — 'exactly'. Trạng từ 'exactly' đi kèm động từ fit: 'fit exactly' = vừa khít hoàn toàn, vừa vặn một cách chính xác. ❌ Bẫy & Phân tích phương án nhiễu: - B 'absolutely', C 'completely', D 'totally' đều mang nghĩa hoàn toàn, nhưng không tạo thành collocation tự nhiên với động từ 'fit' để mô tả sự chính xác kích cỡ như 'exactly'. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 71. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: A. exactly. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze

questions.explanation: Chọn D — 'cause'. Collocation 'cause blisters' = gây ra vết phồng rộp (cho da chân). Bàn chân đi giày vừa khít sẽ ít có nguy cơ bị phồng rộp da. ❌ Bẫy & Phân tích phương án nhiễu: - A 'provoke' = kích động, khiêu khích. - B 'form' = hình thành (blisters có thể form, nhưng đôi giày là tác nhân ngoài nên phải dùng cause blisters, chứ đôi giày không tự form blisters). - C 'initiate' = khởi xướng, bắt đầu một quy trình. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 71. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. questions.explanation: Chọn B — 'performance'. 'Improve performance' = cải thiện thành tích/hiệu suất thi đấu (đặc biệt đối với các vận động viên thể thao). ❌ Bẫy & Phân tích phương án nhiễu: - A 'achievement' = thành tựu (thường là kết quả lớn lao cuối cùng, không dùng phổ biến trong cụm cải thiện tức thời khi chạy như performance). - C 'success' = sự thành công. - D 'winning' = việc chiến thắng. Blue whales

Blue whales, the world's largest animals, have been sighted in British waters (0) FOR the first time in at least twenty years. A mother and calf were spotted (13) ..... least twenty years after the species was thought to have disappeared from the region.

The whales were seen by marine biologists searching the seas to the west (14) ..... Scotland. The scientists, (15) ..... surveillance system detects the sounds made by whales, had been tracking the pair for several days. Among (16) ....., Carol Booker, (17) ..... actually seen a blue whale there herself. She has no doubt about what she saw, because they have distinctive fins which are very small for (18) ..... size.

She says, ‘Worldwide they were almost extinct and (19) ..... seemed they had completely vanished from the North Atlantic, so you can imagine how I felt actually seeing one! However, it is certainly (20) ..... soon to say if it is an indication of a population recovery.’ She goes (21) ..... to say, 'What (22) ..... clear is the importance of this area of the ocean for whales, and (23) ..... essential it is to control pollution of the seas.'

Bigger than (24) ..... dinosaur known to man, blue whales are the largest animals ever to (25) ..... lived on earth. A blue whale is more than six metres long at birth and, (26) ..... fully grown, its heart is the (27) ..... height as a tall man and weighs as much as a horse.

Marie Curie

Marie Curie’s (0) childhood was spent in Poland. She had a passion for learning and she was full of (28) ..... for her studies. Despite being poor, she did (29) ..... well at university and went on to make discoveries which were of great scientific (30) ..... . questions.instruction:
Q38.
• We'll have to _______ whether or not to let you go to the party; it all depends on how you behave between now and then!
• I'm going to _______ Lucia later so I'll pass on a message if you want.
• I can _______ your point but I'm afraid I can't agree with you.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: SEE (xem, gặp, hiểu). • Câu 1: 'We'll have to see whether...' = Chúng ta sẽ phải xem/chờ xem liệu... (quyết định dựa vào tình huống sau). • Câu 2: 'going to see Lucia' = gặp Lucia. • Câu 3: 'see your point' = hiểu quan điểm của bạn. 💡 Mẹo: Từ đa nghĩa phổ biến nhất. 'See' không chỉ là nhìn bằng mắt mà có nghĩa bóng là hiểu (I see) hoặc gặp gỡ (see a doctor/friend). Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 74. Method: complete the gap with "SEE" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure. questions.instruction:
Q39.
• If Lisa can't behave _______ during the swimming lesson she'll have to leave the pool.
• I've never actually met Ramon myself but everyone speaks very _______ of him.
• The new history teacher's really good; he seems to know his subject _______ .
questions.explanation: Đáp án: WELL (tốt, tốt đẹp, thấu đáo). • Câu 1: 'behave well' = cư xử tốt/ngoan ngoãn. • Câu 2: 'speaks very well of him' = mọi người đánh giá rất cao/nói tốt về anh ấy. • Câu 3: 'know his subject well' = hiểu rõ/nắm vững môn học của mình. 💡 Mẹo: WELL vừa là trạng từ chỉ chất lượng (tốt), vừa nằm trong các thành ngữ diễn đạt sự tôn trọng hoặc mức độ hiểu biết chuyên sâu. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 74. Method: complete the gap with "WELL" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure. Language rule: key word transformation keeps the key word unchanged while preserving meaning through grammar pattern and lexical control.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write one word which fits all three sentences. Q39. • If Lisa can't behave _______ during the swimming lesson she'll .... Correct completion: WELL.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 4: Gapped Sentences

questions.instruction:
Q40.
• John’s inexperience might _______ against him if he applies for the manager’s job.
• I can always _______ on Henry to help out in an emergency.
• Mary should _______ herself lucky not to have been sacked for arriving late so often.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: COUNT (coi là, bất lợi, tin cậy). • Câu 1: 'count against him' = là một điểm bất lợi chống lại anh ấy. • Câu 2: 'count on Henry' = tin cậy/dựa dẫm vào Henry. • Câu 3: 'count herself lucky' = tự thấy bản thân mình may mắn. 💡 Mẹo: Động từ 'count' có nghĩa là đếm số, nhưng khi đi với giới từ/cụm từ sẽ biến đổi nghĩa: count on (phrasal verb), count against (gây bất lợi), count oneself as (coi bản thân như). Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 74. Method: complete the gap with "COUNT" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write one word which fits all three sentences. Q40. • John’s inexperience might _______ against him if he applies for.... Correct completion: COUNT.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 4: Gapped Sentences

questions.instruction:
Q41.
• The football team were in _______ spirits before the match and felt sure they would win.
• Mr Prasad has held a _______ position in local government for fifteen years.
• Today’s cycle race may be cancelled because of the _______ winds.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: HIGH (cao, mạnh, phấn chấn). • Câu 1: 'in high spirits' = tinh thần phấn chấn/hào hứng cao độ. • Câu 2: 'held a high position' = giữ chức vụ/vị trí cao trong chính quyền. • Câu 3: 'high winds' = những cơn gió rất mạnh. 💡 Mẹo: HIGH đi kèm với 'spirits' tạo thành ngữ chỉ sự hào hứng, còn khi đi với 'winds' chỉ sức gió mạnh (không dùng strong winds ở đây nếu muốn chung cả 3 câu, dù strong winds cũng đúng ngữ pháp riêng lẻ). Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 75. Method: complete the gap with "HIGH" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write one word which fits all three sentences. Q41. • The football team were in _______ spirits before the match and .... Correct completion: HIGH.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 4: Gapped Sentences

questions.instruction:
Q42.
• The committee _______ to discuss the plans for widening the road.
• The writer _______ the deadline but with only a couple of hours to spare.
• The engineers _______ so many problems that they had to abandon the project.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: MET (quá khứ của MEET: gặp gỡ, đáp ứng, gặp phải). • Câu 1: 'The committee met' = Ủy ban đã họp/gặp nhau. • Câu 2: 'met the deadline' = hoàn thành đúng thời hạn (kịp deadline). • Câu 3: 'met so many problems' = gặp phải/đối mặt với rất nhiều khó khăn. ⚠️ Bẫy: Hãy chú ý thì của câu. Cả ba câu đều ở thì quá khứ (were, had to, widening plans) nên động từ bắt buộc phải chia ở quá khứ: MEET → MET. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 75. Method: complete the gap with "MET" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write one word which fits all three sentences. Q42. • The committee _______ to discuss the plans for widening the roa.... Correct completion: MET.

questions.instruction:
Q43. The company is almost certain to get the contract.
EVERY
The company stands _______ the contract.
acceptedAnswers: winning'. Không dùng dạng nguyên mẫu sau of.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: EVERY CHANCE OF GETTING (hoặc EVERY CHANCE OF WINNING). 📐 Cấu trúc: 'stand every chance of + V-ing' = có mọi cơ hội để đạt được điều gì (rất có khả năng thành công). • Câu gốc: 'almost certain to get' = gần như chắc chắn sẽ có được. • Chuyển đổi: 'stands every chance of getting/winning'. ⚠️ Bẫy: Sau giới từ 'of' bắt buộc động từ phải ở dạng V-ing ('getting/winning'). Không dùng dạng nguyên mẫu sau of. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 76. Method: complete the gap with "EVERY CHANCE OF GETTING" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.

questions.instruction:
Q44. Julia says that her sister is the only person to know of our plans to get married.
APART
Julia says that _______ knows of our plans to get married.
acceptedAnswers: NO ONE APART FROM HER SISTER
APART FROM HER SISTER NO ONE
no one apart from her sister' và 'apart from her sister nobody
no one' đều được chấp nhận.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: NOBODY APART FROM HER SISTER (hoặc NO ONE APART FROM HER SISTER). 📐 Cấu trúc: 'apart from' = ngoại trừ, ngoài... ra. Kết hợp với đại từ phủ định 'nobody/no one' để diễn đạt ý 'không ai ngoại trừ...'. • Câu gốc: 'her sister is the only person to know' = chị gái cô ấy là người duy nhất biết. • Chuyển đổi: 'nobody/no one apart from her sister knows' (không ai ngoài chị gái cô ấy biết). 💡 Lưu ý: Cả thứ tự 'nobody/no one apart from her sister' và 'apart from her sister nobody/no one' đều được chấp nhận. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 76. Method: complete the gap with "NOBODY APART FROM HER SISTER" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write the missing words (3–6 words including the key word). Q44. Julia says that her sister is the only person to kno.... Correct completion: NOBODY APART FROM HER SISTER.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 5: Key Word Transformation

questions.instruction:
Q45. Sven would have called yesterday if he had had more time.
SHORT
Sven didn’t call yesterday _______ time.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: BECAUSE HE WAS SHORT OF (hoặc AS / SINCE HE WAS SHORT OF). 📐 Cấu trúc: 'be short of something' = thiếu cái gì. Kết hợp liên từ chỉ nguyên nhân (because/as/since) + mệnh đề. • Câu gốc: 'if he had had more time' = nếu anh ấy có nhiều thời gian hơn (Sven không gọi vì thiếu thời gian). • Chuyển đổi: 'because he was short of' = bởi vì anh ấy bị thiếu hụt (thời gian). ⚠️ Bẫy: Hãy chú ý thì của câu. Sven không gọi 'yesterday' (quá khứ) nên tobe phải chia ở quá khứ đơn: 'he was short of' chứ không phải 'he is'. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 76. Method: complete the gap with "BECAUSE HE WAS SHORT OF" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write the missing words (3–6 words including the key word). Q45. Sven would have called yesterday if he had had more .... Correct completion: BECAUSE HE WAS SHORT OF.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 5: Key Word Transformation

questions.instruction:
Q46. Many people believe that Edilson has the ability to become world judo champion.
CAPABLE
Many people believe that Edilson _______ the world championship in judo.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: IS CAPABLE OF WINNING. 📐 Cấu trúc: 'be capable of + V-ing' = có khả năng làm gì. • Câu gốc: 'has the ability to become world champion' = có khả năng trở thành nhà vô địch. • Chuyển đổi: 'is capable of winning the world championship' = có khả năng giành chức vô địch thế giới. ⚠️ Bẫy: Nhớ chia động từ tobe 'is' cho chủ ngữ số ít Edilson và dùng dạng V-ing ('winning') sau giới từ 'of'. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 77. Method: complete the gap with "IS CAPABLE OF WINNING" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write the missing words (3–6 words including the key word). Q46. Many people believe that Edilson has the ability to .... Correct completion: IS CAPABLE OF WINNING.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 5: Key Word Transformation

questions.instruction:
Q47. Hilary was asked to give an explanation for making the mistake.
WHY
Hilary was asked to _______ the mistake.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: EXPLAIN WHY SHE HAD MADE (hoặc EXPLAIN WHY SHE MADE). 📐 Cấu trúc: 'explain + wh-clause' = giải thích lý do tại sao... Đổi từ danh từ 'give an explanation' thành động từ 'explain'. • Câu gốc: 'give an explanation for making the mistake' = đưa ra lời giải thích cho việc phạm lỗi. • Chuyển đổi: 'explain why she had made/made the mistake' = giải thích lý do tại sao cô ấy đã phạm sai lầm đó. 💡 Mẹo: Việc phạm sai lầm xảy ra trước yêu cầu giải thích, do đó dùng thì quá khứ hoàn thành 'had made' là chuẩn xác nhất, tuy nhiên quá khứ đơn 'made' cũng được chấp nhận rộng rãi. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 77. Method: complete the gap with "EXPLAIN WHY SHE HAD MADE" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write the missing words (3–6 words including the key word). Q47. Hilary was asked to give an explanation for making t.... Correct completion: EXPLAIN WHY SHE HAD MADE.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 5: Key Word Transformation

questions.instruction:
Q48. In his speech, the Principal did not mention the new language courses.
REFERENCE
In his speech, the Principal _______ the new language courses.
acceptedAnswers: DID NOT MAKE ANY REFERENCE TO
questions.explanation: Đáp án: MADE NO REFERENCE TO (hoặc DID NOT MAKE ANY REFERENCE TO). 📐 Cấu trúc: 'make reference to something' = đề cập/tham chiếu đến cái gì. Tương đương động từ 'mention'. • Câu gốc: 'did not mention' = đã không đề cập đến (phủ định quá khứ). • Chuyển đổi: 'made no reference to' hoặc 'did not make reference to'. ⚠️ Bẫy: Thí sinh cần nhớ động từ đi kèm là 'make' và phải chia ở quá khứ: 'made' hoặc dùng trợ động từ 'did not make'. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 77. Method: complete the gap with "MADE NO REFERENCE TO" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: made no reference to / did not make reference to
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 5: Key Word Transformation

questions.instruction:
Q49. Lucy was very pleased to be given an award.
DELIGHT
Much to _______ given the award.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: HER DELIGHT LUCY WAS (hoặc LUCY'S DELIGHT SHE WAS). 📐 Cấu trúc: 'Much to someone's delight' = Trước sự vui mừng/hân hoan tột cùng của ai đó. Cấu trúc đảo trạng ngữ chỉ cảm xúc lên đầu câu. • Câu gốc: 'Lucy was very pleased' = Lucy đã rất vui lòng. • Chuyển đổi: 'Much to her delight, Lucy was given the award' hoặc 'Much to Lucy's delight, she was given...'. ⚠️ Bẫy: Hãy chú ý cấu trúc này yêu cầu mệnh đề chính phải đầy đủ chủ ngữ và động từ phía sau: 'Lucy was...' hoặc 'she was...'. Đừng quên chia bị động 'was given' của câu gốc. Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 77. Method: complete the gap with "HER DELIGHT LUCY WAS" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: her delight Lucy was / Lucy's delight she was

questions.instruction:
Q50. Sam never thought of asking me for my advice.
OCCURRED
It _______ me for my advice.
acceptedAnswers: NEVER OCCURRED TO SAM TO ASK
questions.correctAnswer: NEVER OCCURRED TO SAM TO ASK
questions.explanation: Đáp án: NEVER OCCURRED TO SAM TO ASK. 📐 Cấu trúc: 'It occurs to someone to do something' = Ai đó nảy ra ý định/nghĩ đến việc làm gì. • Câu gốc: 'Sam never thought of asking' = Sam chưa bao giờ nghĩ đến việc hỏi. • Chuyển đổi: 'It never occurred to Sam to ask me' = Điều đó chưa bao giờ xảy đến trong suy nghĩ của Sam để hỏi tôi. ⚠️ Bẫy: Động từ 'occur' gấp đôi chữ 'r' ở dạng quá khứ: OCCURRED. Phải dùng cấu trúc giới từ 'to Sam' + 'to V' (to ask). Source: CAE 3, Test 3, Page 77. Method: complete the gap with "NEVER OCCURRED TO SAM TO ASK" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write the missing words (3–6 words including the key word). Q50. Sam never thought of asking me for my advice. OCCURR.... Correct completion: NEVER OCCURRED TO SAM TO ASK.
